business.headline: Sensex tanks 800 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Stock markets opened sharply lower as banking, metal and IT stocks dragged benchmarks down, while pharma stocks showed resilience amid global uncertainty, high crude prices and persistent foreign investor selling.

Sensex opens over 500 points lower, Nifty below 23,500; IT stocks declineThe S&P BSE Sensex was down by 533.75 points to 75,500.67, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 169.90 points to 23,469.25 as of 9:31 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Early indicators show a weak start for the benchmark indices. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,549.5 at around 07:57 am. This signals that the Nifty 50 is likely to open below Thursday's closing level of 23,639.15.

LIVE: 398 cylinders seized in Madurai hoarding case, two arrestedLPG shortage live updates: Concerns over cooking gas supply are beginning to affect daily life across several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. Top private schools in Delhi have also issued warnings that lunch services could be affected if LPG supplies tighten further. Several malls in the national capital have asked restaurants to operate conservatively and manage operations with about 80% gas supply. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic. C Senthil Rajan, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, said oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day even during the crisis and that supplies remain intact. Police in several states have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and illegal trade in cylinders. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the impact across cities and industries in India.

No fuel shortage in India, supply chains stable: Hardeep Singh Puri in ParliamentSpeaking in Parliament, Union Petroleum and Natural Gas Minister Hardeep Singh Puri said India has adequate fuel stocks, and there is no cause for concern over availability.

Retail inflation rises to 3.21% in February, higher than 2.75% in JanuaryThe slight uptick in inflation was mainly driven by changes in food prices, which carry a significant weight in the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

Tech Mahindra onboarding delay keeps over 1,000 freshers waiting: ReportMany of the affected graduates are from colleges in Maharashtra. They had cleared several stages of the recruitment process earlier in 2025 but are yet to receive formal offer letters or confirmed joining dates.

Sensex ends over 820 points lower, Nifty below 23,700; Coal India gains 5%Markets remained volatile through the session, with the Nifty touching a low of 23,556.30 during the day before trimming some losses by the close. Selling was seen across several heavyweight stocks, particularly in auto, banking and capital goods sectors.

Gulf conflict raises insurance costs for Indian airlines, fares may riseIndustry sources told India Today TV that airline operators are now paying additional insurance charges for every aircraft flying into the Middle East, significantly increasing the cost of operations on these routes.

Stocking LPG cylinders during shortage? Know the legal limits and penaltiesUnder law, a domestic household is generally allowed to keep two LPG cylinders of 14.2 kg each at a time. This usually includes one cylinder connected for use and another spare cylinder kept as backup.

Rupee sinks to record low of Rs 92.36 against US dollarThe currency weakened as global markets reacted to escalating tensions in West Asia and a sharp jump in oil prices, with Brent crude climbing above $100 per barrel.

Brent crude back above $100 per barrel: Why are oil prices rising again?Brent crude has crossed $100 again. With tanker attacks and rising war tensions threatening supply routes, the big question now is how long oil prices will stay this high.

Bank closures reported across Gulf after Iran warns civilians to stay 1 km awayIran's warning came after reports that an administrative building linked to Bank Sepah, one of Iran's largest state-owned banks, was hit overnight in Tehran.

Sensex down 800 points: What's behind today's market bloodbath?The Sensex and Nifty extended their sell-off on Thursday as oil prices surged amid West Asia tensions, weak global cues persisted and foreign investors continued selling, keeping pressure on Indian equities.
Sensex crashes 900 points, Nifty below 23,600; Eternal down 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down by 938.41 points to 75,925.30, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 305.55 points to 23,561.30 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again today?Early signals from GIFT Nifty suggest that the benchmark indices could open lower when trading begins. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,784.50 at around 7:58 am. This indicates that the Nifty 50 may open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,866.85.

Highlights: Top private school in Delhi warns of 'no lunch' risk amid LPG shortageLPG crisis live highlights: The impact of the LPG supply disruption is now being felt by India Inc as well, with some companies reporting operational issues due to cooking gas shortages affecting cafeterias and food services. At the same time, disruptions in commercial LPG supply have been reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, with restaurants and eateries struggling to procure cylinders and in some cases cutting menus or scaling down operations. Police in several states have also stepped up monitoring amid rumours around LPG supply, warning of strict action against misinformation, hoarding and theft of cylinders. The government, however, maintains there is no nationwide LPG crisis and says refinery output has been increased by about 10% to stabilise supplies. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government response and the situation across Indian cities.

India's Russian oil imports jump 50% in March as Middle East war hits energy supplyIndia currently consumes about 5.8 million barrels of crude oil per day, with nearly 88 per cent of that demand met through imports. A large portion of these supplies typically comes from Middle Eastern producers such as Saudi Arabia, Iraq and the United Arab Emirates.

Stock market crash? Why stopping your SIPs in panic is a bad ideaWhen markets fall sharply, fear often takes over investing decisions. But did you know continuing SIPs during market crashes has historically rewarded disciplined investors?

Centre procures more LPG, blames misinformation for panic-buyingIndia's domestic LPG production has increased by 25% following government orders to boost output, officials said at a joint briefing by the Ministry of Shipping, Ministry of External Affairs, and Ministry of Petroleum.

entertainment.headline: Kapoor family copyrights Rishi Kapoor's name posthumously to prevent misuse The Kapoor family has taken legal steps to protect the name and legacy of late actor Rishi Kapoor. This move ensures no unauthorised commercial use of his name without family's permission.

Neena Gupta's Badhai Ho moment at 66? Actor reacts to rumours Did Neena Gupta spark pregnancy rumours at 66? The actor finally addressed viral speculation after her appearance at a high-profile wedding event.

Late Rob Reiner to be honoured at Oscars 2026, confirms Conan O'Brien The 98th Academy Awards will honour the late Rob Reiner with a powerful tribute, following his and his wife's death in December. Host Conan O'Brien and producers have shared their thoughts on the upcoming ceremony and its plans to remember industry legends.

Hollywood, not Bollywood: Revanth Reddy's vision for Allu Arjun and Telugu cinema Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy encouraged Allu Arjun to explore opportunities in Hollywood and urged the Telugu industry to aim for a global presence beyond regional industries. He attended the inauguration of Allu Cinemas in Hyderabad.

Harry Styles' American Girls feels like One Direction throwback. Here's why Harry Styles' American Girls from Kiss All The Time. Disco, Occasionally. has sparked huge One Direction nostalgia. Many fans believed the music video cleverly mirrors the iconic Kiss You era.

Bigg Boss 19: Gaurav Khanna confirms receiving Rs 50 lakh prize After online speculation about his prize money, television actor Gaurav Khanna confirmed he has received the full amount from Bigg Boss 19. The winner addressed how his reality show victory has changed the way audiences recognise him.

Radikaa's grandmother act and Rs 50 crore-run: Thaai Kizhavi shakes Tamil box office Thaai Kizhavi, a rural drama starring Radikaa Sarathkumar as a sharp-witted grandmother, has become one of Tamil cinema's most profitable films in 2026. Its success challenges traditional Kollywood formulas by focusing on strong women characters and profit-sharing with cast and crew.

Dhurandhar could've gone bigger with Telugu dub, lost big opportunity: Allu Sirish Actor Allu Sirish urged Hindi film studios to dub major releases in regional languages, saying missed opportunities affect wider reach. He pointed to the success of Animal and the missed chance with Dhurandhar.

Netflix confirms KPop Demon Hunters sequel after global success Netflix and Sony Pictures Animation have announced a sequel to the animated hit KPop Demon Hunters. The release date has not yet been confirmed.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 new teaser: Miranda Priestly reminds Andy who's the boss A new teaser for The Devil Wears Prada 2 offered a glimpse of Miranda Priestly and Andy Sachs reuniting nearly 20 years after the original film. It also introduced Indian origin actor Simone Ashley's character to the fans.

The Khoslas are back: Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 gets a release date. See it here The makers of Khosla Ka Ghosla 2 have confirmed the sequel will release on the occasion of Raksha Bandhan this year. It would mark the return of some members of the original cast after two decades with new additions.

Sunny Deol teams up with Akhanda director Boyapati Srinu for Jaat 2: Report Actor Sunny Deol, who recently tasted success with Border 2, may soon collaborate with Telugu filmmaker Boyapati Srinu for the sequel of his recent film, Jaat.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan faces fresh setback as Amazon Prime exits OTT deal: Report Amid delays surrounding Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan, reports suggest Amazon Prime Video has withdrawn from its OTT deal.

Hansika Motwani shares first post after divorce from Sohael Khaturiya Actor Hansika Motwani broke her silence online after being granted a divorce from businessman Sohael Khaturiya. The couple ended their marriage after four years.

Ektaa Kapoor recalls family fights over bold content: Had to hear 100 taunts Ektaa Kapoor revealed that her parents, Jeetendra and Shobha Kapoor, strongly opposed her launch of ALTT due to its bold content. The producer said she faced 100 taunts at home. The platform was eventually banned by the Indian government.

Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Aspirants Season 3, The Taj Story and more This week's OTT releases on Prime Video, JioHotstar and Lionsgate Play bring a mix of drama, comedy and inspiring stories. Titles like Aspirants: Season 3, The Taj Story and The Madison headline the new lineup.

Children are choking: Deepika urges BMC to tackle Mumbai's alarming AQI levels Deepika Padukone has called on Mumbai's Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) to take action over the city's deteriorating air quality. She expressed concern for citizens and children as the city faces a severe heatwave.

South exhibitors propose mandatory 8-week theatrical run before OTT release Exhibitors from five southern states met in Bengaluru and proposed, in principle, to enforce a standardised theatrical window for films before their digital release.

Netflix, ZEE5, SonyLiv this week: Made In Korea, Andha Pyaar 2 and more This week's OTT lineup brings a mix of new series, films and reality shows across Netflix, ZEE5, SonyLiv and JioHotstar. Titles such as Made In Korea, Andha Pyaar 2 and Virgin River: Season 7 are among the key releases.

general news.headline: Iran says struck US aircraft carrier Lincoln, American report says threat foiledWhile the Iranian Navy claimed that it used advanced missiles and drones to target the US vessel, forcing it to retract, the US forces said that they fired at a ship approaching the aircraft carrier, without specifying further details.

US partly lifts Russia oil sanctions amid Iran war days after India waiverCountries can buy Russian oil for 30 days after the US allowed sale of shipments already at sea to ease supply disruptions caused by the Iran war.

Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei lost a leg, is in coma: ReportA report claims Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei lost a leg and is in a coma after an airstrike that killed his father, raising questions about who is directing the war.

US military aircraft with 5 aboard crashes in Iraq, Iran-backed group claims roleA US KC-135 refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq during Operation Epic Fury, prompting rescue efforts. Officials said the incident was not caused by hostile fire, while another aircraft landed safely.

Who is Ali Jafari, the mastermind who made Iran's defeat impossible?Despite the US wiping out Iran's top leadership on the first day of the strikes, Tehran continues to fight on fiercely. This is due to the Mosaic Defence Doctrine of Mohammad Ali Jafari. Jafari took lessons from the US invasion of Iraq in 2003, and ensured Tehran would remain resilient even if its top leadership was taken out.

LIVE: Congress protests LPG shortage, carries clay stoves to BJP HQLPG shortage live updates: Concerns over cooking gas supply are beginning to affect daily life across several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. Top private schools in Delhi have also issued warnings that lunch services could be affected if LPG supplies tighten further. Several malls in the national capital have asked restaurants to operate conservatively and manage operations with about 80% gas supply. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic. C Senthil Rajan, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, said oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day even during the crisis and that supplies remain intact. Police in several states have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and illegal trade in cylinders. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the impact across cities and industries in India.

Sergio Gor, Akshay Kumar to Sanju Samson: Big voices line up for Conclave 2026The India Today Conclave 2026 returns this year amid a global churn and turmoil in the Middle East, with a wide spectrum of personalities, ranging from politics and diplomacy to entertainment and sports, set to reflect on challenges and topics that will define India's future.

Iranian girls' school hit in strike had active website, years-long online presenceA girls' school in Minab, Iran, with a long online presence and visible school activity, was destroyed in a February 28 strike that investigators believe was likely carried out by the United States.

Kritika Kamra-Gaurav Kapur reception: Farhan Akhtar to Malaika Arora, celebs attendAttending the reception were several notable personalities from the worlds of entertainment and sport. Cricket legend Sachin Tendulkar arrived with Anjali Tendulkar, while actors Richa Chadha, Ali Fazal, Malaika Arora and Farhan Akhtar, among others were also present.

Live: US, Israeli strikes rock Tehran, residents say homes shook by blastsUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran war entered day 14. New Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, issued his first statement since the conflict began on February 28. In the message released by Tehran, he warned that US bases in the region will be attacked. Conflicting reports also emerged about his condition, with some suggesting he was wounded during the initial US-Israeli strikes that killed his father, former supreme leader Ali Khamenei. Meanwhile, Iran continued launching missile and drone attacks across the Gulf region, triggering air defence responses in several countries. Sirens sounded again in Doha as regional forces intercepted incoming projectiles, while the UAE and Saudi Arabia said they shot down multiple ballistic missiles and drones. Tehran has also targeted shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil supplies. Israel, in turn, intensified strikes on Iranian-linked targets, including Basij forces in Tehran and Hezbollah-linked sites in Beirut.

Like Covid time, we will overcome this too: PM Modi on LPG crisisThe prime minister also cautioned against attempts to spread panic about LPG availability and warned that strict action would be taken against black-marketing and hoarding. He also urged state governments to strengthen monitoring mechanisms to prevent unscrupulous elements from exploiting the situation.

No love jihad or conversion: Kumbh fame Monalisa, husband shut criticismSpeaking to reporters after the ceremony, the couple emphasised that their relationship was built on mutual respect and that religion had never been a condition for their marriage.

Dhurandhar could've gone bigger with Telugu dub, lost big opportunity: Allu SirishActor Allu Sirish urged Hindi film studios to dub major releases in regional languages, saying missed opportunities affect wider reach. He pointed to the success of Animal and the missed chance with Dhurandhar.

Google Maps can now answer real-world questionsGoogle is adding a new AI-powered feature to the popular Google Maps app, which is used by millions of Indians. The company has introduced Ask Maps, a conversational tool built using its Gemini AI models that allows users to ask detailed real-world questions and receive direct answers inside the app.

Woman clerk refuses to issue ticket over exact change dispute. Railways reactsA video from Jalandhar Cantt Railway Station showing a disagreement between a passenger and a ticket clerk over payment methods and change has prompted official action.

He's damaged but probably alive: Trump on Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba KhameneiDonald Trump said Iran's new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, was 'damaged but probably alive', as reports suggested he was critically injured in the airstrike that killed his father.

Radikaa's grandmother act and Rs 50 crore-run: Thaai Kizhavi shakes Tamil box officeThaai Kizhavi, a rural drama starring Radikaa Sarathkumar as a sharp-witted grandmother, has become one of Tamil cinema's most profitable films in 2026. Its success challenges traditional Kollywood formulas by focusing on strong women characters and profit-sharing with cast and crew.

Michigan synagogue attacker lost family in Lebanon airstrike days ago: ReportShortly before the Michigan synagogue attack, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali called his ex-wife and told her to take care of their children.

Trump acts on impulse, believed Iran could be another Venezuela: Ex-NSA John BoltonFormer US National Security Adviser John Bolton criticised Donald Trump over the Iran war, saying the President may have misjudged the Iran conflict by believing he could achieve a quick regime change similar to Venezuela. He also warned that disruptions to oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz were predictable risks.

Notice for CEC Gyanesh Kumar's removal to be tabled today, 193 MPs signEarlier, Opposition parties accused Gyanesh Kumar of helping the ruling BJP. The accusations mounted during the time of the Special Intensive Revision of the voter lists.

Hollywood, not Bollywood: Revanth Reddy's vision for Allu Arjun and Telugu cinemaTelangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy encouraged Allu Arjun to explore opportunities in Hollywood and urged the Telugu industry to aim for a global presence beyond regional industries. He attended the inauguration of Allu Cinemas in Hyderabad.

Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen is stepping down after 18 yearsAdobe has announced that Shantanu Narayen will be stepping down as CEO. Following this announcement, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella shared a special post for Narayen, praising the latter's role in shaping Adobe as a major software company.

Pentagon reveals why US Military has banned Claude, labelled Anthropic as supply riskUS Defense Undersecretary Emil Michael has claimed that Anthropic's Claude AI model could pollute the Pentagon's supply chain. His comments come at a time when the AI startup is suing the US Department of Defense for being designated as a supply chain risk. Here is everything you need to know.

Nasa to launch Artemis-II mission to the Moon on April 1Nasa said preparations are continuing at the Kennedy Space Centre, where engineers are completing final work on the rocket and spacecraft inside the Vehicle Assembly Building.

sports.headline: Suit, stocks and squash: Ramit Tandon now eyes the Olympic dream for India Swapping the world of finance for the Squash court, Ramit Tandon now eyes the Olympic dream for India. In an exclusive with India Today, the World No.38 talks about his journey from the cubicle to chasing his love for the sport, major goals in 2026 and more.

England lifts media ban on Ashes stars despite ongoing performance review ECB has lifted the temporary restriction on centrally contracted England players speaking to the media. The move comes amid scrutiny following England's Ashes defeat and a series of off-field controversies involving players during recent tours.

Sunrisers Leeds X account suspended after backlash over Abrar Ahmed Hundred signing Hours after making the signing of Abrar Ahmed during the Hundred auction, Sunrisers Leeds' X account was suspended on Thursday, March 12. The Leeds team is fully owned by the Sun Group, who are the owners of Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL.

South Africa the stupidest team of T20 WC, could've knocked out India: Vaughan T20 World Cup: Michael Vaughan labelled South Africa the stupidest team during the recently-concluded T20 World Cup 2026. Vaughan said that South Africa should have lost their Super 8 clash against the West Indies to put India on the brink of elimination during the second phase of the tournament.

Duplantis breaks pole vault world record for 15th time at Uppsala World Athletics Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis soared to another historic milestone, breaking the pole vault world record for the 15th time. The Swedish superstar cleared 6.31m at the Mondo Classic in Uppsala.

MS Dhoni needs to bat higher than No.8 for CSK in IPL 2026: Pujara Cheteshwar Pujara feels MS Dhoni should bat higher for CSK in IPL 2026. The former India batter believes the Chennai Super Kings legend can still change matches if he gets more time at the crease.

Trump says Iran should skip FIFA World Cup for their safety, life Iran's sports minister said on Wednesday that it was not possible for his nation's athletes to participate after the US launched airstrikes alongside Israel against Tehran. The attacks triggered a region-wide conflict that has shown no signs of abating.

Indian Wells: Djokovic admits bitter feeling after losing pre-quarters to Draper World No. 3 Novak Djokovic lost to defending champion Jack Draper in a fiercely-fought Round of 16 match that lasted 2 hours and 37 minutes. Djokovic won the opening set, but he went down 4-6, 6-4, 7-6(5) in California on Thursday.

Boycott SRH in IPL: SunRisers face backlash for buying Pak spinner in Hundred A section of fans has called for a boycott of SunRisers Hyderabad after SunRisers Leeds, one of four Indian-owned franchises in The Hundred, signed Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed in Thursday's auction.

Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma likely to attend Kuldeep Yadav-Vanshika wedding India cricketer Kuldeep Yadav will marry his longtime girlfriend Vanshika in a private ceremony in Mussoorie on Saturday, March 14, with Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma among the high-profile guests expected to attend.

Tendulkar was shocked when team began looking for replacement: Former selector Former BCCI chief selector Sandip Patil has revealed that legendary India batter Sachin Tendulkar was left shocked when the team management began looking for his replacement in 2012.

The Hundred auction: Indian-owned SunRisers Leeds buy Pakistan's Abrar AhmedThe Hundred Auction 2026: Indian-owned SunRisers Leeds bought Pakistan spinner Abrar Ahmed for £190,000 on Thursday, March 12. It marked the first time in a long while that a team owned by an Indian Premier League franchise has picked a Pakistan cricketer.

Jasprit Bumrah's Test credentials questioned by Alastair Cook after World Cup heroicsFormer England captain Alastair Cook has questioned India fast bowler Jasprit Bumrah's Test credentials following his heroics in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Yuki Bhambri reaches Indian Wells semifinals in spirited run with Andre GoranssonIndia's top-ranked doubles player Yuki Bhamri and his Swedish partner Andre Goransson have reached the semifinal of Indian Wells, a Masters 1000 tournament in California.

Virat Kohli will be hungrier than ever to win successive IPL trophies: Irfan PathanFormer India all-rounder Irfan Pathan has said that Virat Kohli will be hungrier than ever to win back-to-back IPL trophies. Kohli will return to action in the Indian Premier League 2026 (IPL 2026) for Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB).

Stranded South Africa, West Indies squads leave India after travel chaosRelief finally arrived for the stranded squads in Kolkata. South Africa and West Indies players have now begun their journeys home after days of travel uncertainty following the T20 World Cup.

Rahul Dravid to be honoured with BCCI lifetime achievement award on March 15The Board of Control for Cricket in India is set to honour Rahul Dravid with the Col. C. K. Nayudu Lifetime Achievement Award for his contribution to Indian cricket. Dravid is the first cricketer since Sachin Tendulkar in 2024 to receive the honour.

Dropped from T20 World Cup, BCCI set to name Shubman Gill Cricketer of the YearIndia's ODI and Test captain, Shubman Gill is reportedly going to receive BCCI's Cricketer of the Year award during their annual Naman awards later this month. This title comes to Gill after getting dropped from India's T20 World Cup squad.

Another World Cup dream slips through the cracks of boardroom incompetenceIndia's Women's Asian Cup exit was more than a defeat. Administrative chaos, from ill-fitting jerseys to poor preparation, pushed another World Cup dream out of reach.

What did Gambhir tell Indian team after T20 World Cup win? Suryakumar revealsT20 World Cup final: India captain Suryakumar Yadav revealed what Gautam Gambhir told the players inside the dressing room after they won the title. India defeated New Zealand by 96 runs to secure their third T20 World Cup crown.

Greatest ODI game ever? South Africa chase down 434 against AustraliaOn this day in 2006, South Africa completed the greatest chase in the history of ODIs as they defeated Australia after being set a target of 435 runs. The Proteas scored 438 and won the match by one wicket in Johannesburg.

Vinicius Jr joins Cristiano Ronaldo on elite Real Madrid list after Man City winAnother Champions League night, another Real Madrid record rewritten. Vinicius Jr joined Cristiano Ronaldo on an elite assist list as Madrid dismantled Manchester City at the Bernabeu.

Mohsin Naqvi promises jobs, medical aid to Pakistan hockey team after WC berthPakistan cricket board chairman Mohsin Naqvi promised jobs and medical support to the Pakistan hockey team players after they qualified for the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026. Pakistan made it to the tournament after a gap of eight years.

India are now on a different level: Viv Richards praises white-ball dominanceIndia's recent run in global white-ball tournaments has drawn praise from one of cricket's greatest icons. Viv Richards said the team's dominance in ODIs and T20Is has taken the modern limited-overs game to a new level.

Watch: Dube presents T20 World Cup medal to his father, calls him his real heroShivam Dube won hearts on social media by placing his T20 World Cup 2026 winner's medal around his father's neck after India's title win. Dube hailed his father as his real hero in the Instagram post.

A trophy is a trophy: Why India's T20 World Cup win is no lesser prizeIndia's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph has triggered familiar attempts to diminish the format and the achievement. Yet conquering a 20-team tournament in cricket's most volatile format demands extraordinary precision, making India's dominance impossible to dismiss.

Dropping Axar Patel in IND vs SA was my mistake, I apologised: Suryakumar YadavT20 World Cup: India's only defeat in their triumphant T20 World Cup 2026 campaign came with a selection call that later haunted the team management. Captain Suryakumar Yadav has now admitted dropping Axar Patel for the South Africa clash was his mistake.

Will Iran be replaced at the World Cup after withdrawal threat? FIFA ponders optionsAfter Iran's withdrawal threat from the World Cup 2026, FIFA are weighing up their options to replace them in the tournament. Iraq and UAE are being considered as replacements by the international governing body.

Ahead of IPL 2026, Dhoni cleared of conflict of interest allegations by BCCIBCCI Ethics Officer Justice Arun Mishra has dismissed a complaint against MS Dhoni, ruling no conflict of interest related to his role in the IPL and cricket academy ties. The order clarifies that Dhoni's continued participation as a player does not violate BCCI conflict-of-interest regulations.

Pune lawyer alleges Hardik Pandya insulted national flag, files police complaintIndia's T20 World Cup celebrations have landed in legal controversy after a Pune lawyer accused Hardik Pandya of disrespecting the national flag during the on-field festivities. The complainant has urged police to register an FIR over the alleged incident.

Fede Valverde relishing hat-trick as Real Madrid stun Man City: Best game of my lifeFederico Valverde said he experienced the best night of his career after scoring a stunning hat-trick to lead Real Madrid to a 3-0 win over Manchester City in the Champions League round of 16. The Uruguayan struck three times in the first half at the Santiago Bernabeu.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
